reasoning: The 1500m freestyle long course world record is broken very rarely (typically holding for around 10 years, as seen with Sun Yang and Grant Hackett), and nearly always at peak summer competitions like the Olympics or World Aquatics Championships. Therefore, the chance of another swimmer breaking this record or Finke losing it to a doping infraction in this short and off-peak window is exceedingly low. Probabilities slightly decay to account for growing uncertainty over a longer horizon.
